clad.def: (clothe的過去分詞)穿衣的、被覆蓋的的人的人
clad.ex: The snow-clad mountain peaks looked truly stunning tonight.

cladding.ex: The modern building has shiny aluminum exterior cladding.

claim.def: 聲稱、主張、索賠、要求、宣稱的人的人物
claim.col: stake a claim, lay claim to, insurance claim, baggage claim
claim.ex: You need to make an insurance claim for the storm damage.

claimant.def: 索取者、提出要求的人人物 (單數)
claimant.ex: The court decided that he was the only rightful claimant.

claimed.def: 聲稱了的、被索取的人的人 (或物)
claimed.ex: The rebels claimed victory after a long and hard battle.

claiming.def: 索求、主張、聲稱的人 (或物)
claiming.ex: A local group is claiming responsibility for the protest.

clairvoyance.def: 千里眼、洞察力的人物 (單數)
clairvoyance.ex: She claims to possess clairvoyance and can see the future.

clairvoyant.def: 透視的、有洞察力的的人的人物 (單數)
clairvoyant.ex: The clairvoyant vision helped the investigators find the car.

clam.def: 蛤、蚌、(口)沉默寡言的人人物 (單數)
clam.col: clam bake, clam up (閉嘴), fried clams
clam.ex: We enjoyed fresh fried clams on the beach for our dinner.

clamber.ex: They managed to clamber up the rocks to reach the cave.

clambering.def: 正在攀爬的、困難地爬的人 (或物)
clambering.ex: The excited clambering child reached the top of the slide.

clammy.def: 濕冷的、黏糊的人人物 (黏)
clammy.ex: His clammy hands showed that he was very nervous tonight.

clamor/clamour.def: 喧鬧、喧囂、大聲要求的人物 (單數)
clamor/clamour.ex: There was a lot of public clamor for a new local park.

clamorous.def: 大聲要求的、吵開的人的人物
clamorous.ex: The meeting was interrupted by a loud and clamorous protest.

clamp.def: 夾具、鉗子、夾緊的人的人物
clamp.col: clamp down, metal clamp, wheel clamp
clamp.ex: Use a metal clamp to hold the two pieces of wood together.

clamping.def: 夾緊、固定、取締的人 (或物)
clamping.ex: The machine uses a strong clamping force to hold the part.

clan.def: 一族、宗族、集團的人的人物 (單數)
clan.ex: He belongs to a famous Scottish clan with a long history.

clandestine.def: 秘密的、私底下的的人的人物
clandestine.ex: They had a clandestine meeting in a dark corner of the cafe.

clandestinely.ex: The group operated clandestinely for over three years tonight.

clang.def: 叮噹聲、鏗鏘聲的人物 (單數)
clang.ex: The heavy metal gate closed with a loud and sharp clang.

clanger.ex: He really dropped a clanger when he forgot the boss's name.

clanging.def: 叮噹響的、發鏗鏘聲的人 (或物)
clanging.ex: We were woken by the clanging noise of the trash bins.

clangor.def: 鏗鏘聲、叮噹聲人物 (單數)
clangor.ex: The industrial clangor of the factory filled the entire hall.

clank.def: 叮噹聲、發出叮鐺聲的人的人物
clank.ex: We could hear the slow and heavy clank of the iron chains.

clanking.def: 叮噹響的、鏗鏘的人 (或物)
clanking.ex: The noisy clanking machine was in need of some oil today.

clannish.def: 宗族的、排他的的人人物
clannish.ex: The villagers were very clannish and suspicious of strangers.
